脗聽脗聽Waldemar Tomaszewski (ECR), ra脜隆tu. 芒聙聯 Ponia Pirmininke, 脜隆iandien aptarin脛聴jame labai svarbi脛聟 organizuoto nusikalstamumo tem脛聟, kuri kelia rimt脛聟 gr脛聴sm脛聶 m脜芦s脜鲁 pilie脛聧iams, Europos ekonomikai, valstybin脛聴ms ir vietos vald脜戮ios institucijoms.

Kasmet ES nusikaltim脜鲁 aukomis tampa net 75 mln. 脜戮moni脜鲁. Tod脛聴l reikia atnaujinti Nusikaltim脜鲁 auk脜鲁 teisi脜鲁 direktyv脛聟, kad nusikaltim脜鲁 aukoms b脜芦t脜鲁 suteikta geresn脛聴 pagalba, veiksmingesnis teisingumas ir kompensacija, atlyginanti patirt脛聟 脜戮al脛聟. Didelis d脛聴mesys tur脛聴t脜鲁 b脜芦ti skiriamas nusikalstam脜鲁 tinkl脜鲁, u脜戮siiman脛聧i脜鲁 prekyba 脜戮mon脛聴mis, i脜隆ardymui, ypating脛聟 d脛聴mes脛炉 skiriant nepilname脛聧ius i脜隆naudojantiems asmenims, grasinantiems smurtu aukoms ir j脜鲁 脜隆eimoms bei verbuojantiems aukas internetu. Kova su prievarta prie脜隆 vaikus internete ir u脜戮 jo, 脛炉skaitant med脜戮iagos, kurioje vaizduojama prievarta prie脜隆 vaikus, k脜芦rim脛聟 ir platinim脛聟, tur脛聴t脜鲁 b脜芦ti ai脜隆kiau i脜隆d脛聴styta kei脛聧iant 脛炉statymus. Be to, 脜隆i脜鲁 dien脜鲁 aktualijos reikalauja kovoti su nusikalstamais tinklais, dalyvaujan脛聧iais neteis脛聴tame migrant脜鲁 gabenime, ypa脛聧 tais, kurie si脜芦lo pagalb脛聟 pagrindiniais migracijos keliais vykstant 脛炉 Europos S脛聟jung脛聟. Turime stiprinti kov脛聟 su tokiais veiksmais, nes masinis nelegali脜鲁 imigrant脜鲁 antpl脜芦dis kelia gr脛聴sm脛聶 m脜芦s脜鲁 pilie脛聧i脜鲁 ir 脜隆ali脜鲁 saugumui. Nepamir脜隆kime, kad svarbiausia turi b脜芦ti paprast脜鲁 脜戮moni脜鲁 gerov脛聴 ir saugumas. Ir tai tur脛聴t脜鲁 b脜芦ti 脛炉traukta 脛炉 pakeist脛聟 direktyv脛聟.

脗聽

8. Presentation by the Council of its position on the draft general budget of the European Union for the financial year 2025 - all sections (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber die Erl脙陇uterung des Standpunkts des Rates zum Entwurf des Gesamthaushaltsplans der Europ脙陇ischen Union f脙录r das Haushaltsjahr 2025 durch den Rat 芒聙聯 alle Einzelpl脙陇ne (2024/2713(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽P脙漏ter Ben脜聭 Banai, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members,脗聽Commissioner, I am honoured to present today the Council's position on the draft budget of the European Union for the financial year 2025. We have worked on the Commission's proposal since it was presented on 19脗聽June.

I am pleased to say that after careful reflection, the Council unanimously adopted its well-balanced position on 13脗聽September. The Council's position provides an amount of EUR脗聽191.5脗聽billion in commitment appropriations and EUR脗聽146.2脗聽billion in payment appropriations, not including appropriations foreseen for special instruments outside the Multiannual Financial Framework.

The Council undertook a detailed analysis of the commitment appropriations for each programme and action by budget line, in order to ensure accurate budgeting and support clearly set priorities. It also takes into consideration the consequences of Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine and related or other crises, including migration and border management, humanitarian aid and defence.

The guiding principles of our position are the respect of prudent and realistic budgeting, the need to provide adequate resources for the key priorities in a balanced way throughout the MFF and the creation of sufficient margins under the ceilings to deal with unforeseen circumstances.

Our position includes reinforcements for heading脗聽4, which is migration and border management, heading脗聽5, security and defence and heading脗聽6, neighbourhood and the world, to strengthen key union priorities, support frontline Member States in the current geopolitical context and ensure a sufficient level of humanitarian aid is available in 2025, including for Ukraine, Georgia and Moldova.

However, the Council's position also includes downward adjustments to headings脗聽1 to 6, aiming at reducing certain lines with higher increases compared to the voted budget 2024. The objective is to mitigate the risks in terms of absorption capacity.

Furthermore, the Council also reduced support expenditure as well as a few lines for decentralised agencies at cruising speed, which were increased by more than 2脗聽% when compared to the voted budget 2024.

In addition, a targeted adjustment is proposed for the European Union recovery instrument line under subheading脗聽2b. Our approach with respect to the financing costs of NextGenerationEU is fully in line with the revised MFF regulation, but limits the use of special instruments to preserve sufficient amounts for unforeseen events, as well as for the future needs to cover the overrun interest costs, which are expected to be much larger at the end of the MFF period.

As regards European public administration 芒聙聯 heading脗聽7 芒聙聯 the Council's position is limited to the creation of savings from expenditure related to buildings and allowances. Concerning payment appropriations, the Council proposes a downward correction proportionate to the adjustments in commitment appropriations. I look forward to your comments and the discussion. Thank you very much for your attention.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Johannes Hahn, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members of the European Parliament, dear Secretary of State Banai, I really would like to congratulate the Hungarian Presidency for reaching an agreement in the Council on the draft budget 2025, and to thank Mr Banai for presenting it today.

Of course, I will also defend the Commission's proposal. The draft budget is a balanced proposal to finance the 2025 budget, reflecting the agreement reached in the first ever mid-term revision of the MFF ceilings. This was undoubtedly a collective success, which has brought substantial additional funding for our key shared priorities. The Commission's proposal for the draft budget 2025 and the accompanying financial programming for 2026 and 2027 reflect this revision in full.

Therefore, the draft budget 2025 also translates the difficult choices made in the context of the MFF revision, namely the redeployments in several programmes to finance the new priorities. The proposed cut by the Council by EUR脗聽1.5脗聽billion in commitments goes beyond the agreement reached on the MFF mid-term revision, and is neither necessary nor justified.

In particular, the cuts of EUR脗聽643脗聽million in heading 1 芒聙聯 Single market, innovation and digital 芒聙聯 are at odds with the shared objective to reinforce the Union's competitiveness and EU security when they concern programmes such as Horizon Europe, Safe Digital and Secure Connectivity. Concerning ITER, I think we should wait with possible cuts until we have the actual needs.

On the EURI cascade, the Commission made a balanced proposal, which delivered on the agreed 50脗聽% benchmark for Step 2 and without cuts for existing programmes. The amount will be reviewed in October in the remaining letter, when we will have a final view of the needs for the euro interest line, based on the actual disbursements by the end of the third quarter of this year.

As regards heading 6, we welcome the Council support for the way in which we propose to implement the MFF mid-term revision through redeployments linked to the future decommitments. We take note of the adopted declaration and will continue reporting regularly on decommitments.

As regards heading 7, we appreciate our positions are fairly close. This is a recognition of the efforts made by the Commission and all institutions. We will continue to make such efforts, even if it is clear that the administration will remain under substantial pressure.

Allow me to underline that the Commission makes all efforts to respect stable staffing and to limit the increases on non-salary expenditures for all institutions. Yet without the mid-term review reinforcements we had asked for 芒聙聯 in vain 芒聙聯 on the basis of objective evidence, the mobilisation of the Single Margin Instrument to offset the impact of high inflation on the administrative expenditure of the institutions, this will remain unavoidable. We will update the exact needs in the autumn as we progress in negotiations.

I would like to invite the Council and the European Parliament to consider that, on the one hand, there is no need to go far beyond the agreement on the MFF revision concerning the cuts. And on the other hand, we should maintain some room for manoeuvre for the challenges we already know are coming our way and for those that will come later. That is why we should refrain from proposing reinforcements far beyond the agreement on the MFF revision.

The Commission's amending letter will revise, as appropriate, our proposals for the EURI interest line and on administrative expenditure. It will also include the usual update to agricultural expenditure. Following Parliament's hearing, we will send the usual executability letter as an input to the conciliation and, as always, we will do our best to facilitate the progress and reach a successful outcome on the budgetary files, ideally during this College mandate.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Victor Negrescu, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Minister, Commissioner, colleagues, no 芒聙聯 the proposal that the Council has made for next year's EU budget cannot be accepted as such. It is too far from what the European Parliament has requested to see in its guidelines earlier this year and it proposes severe cuts that go against what has been agreed in the recent revision of the multiannual financial framework.

My role as rapporteur and negotiator on behalf of the European Parliament is to defend the role of our institution as a budgetary authority, but also to make sure that the annual budget addresses the priorities, which we have agreed together with the political groups.

The recent European elections have sent a clear message that for more solidarity between Member States and more investment in policies and programmes, we need adequate financing and we need, of course, to use that financing in order to improve people's lives. This call cannot be translated into more budgetary cuts and a reduction in badly needed resources to help our people go through these difficult times. Prudent budgeting does not mean to block the financing of citizens' priorities. It does not mean rebates, and it certainly does not mean cutting programmes to finance high interest rates. We need to do more together and better, not less.

Indeed, some errors have been made in the forecast, also by the Commission. It is up to us to find together the best solutions to the current challenges, but we still have a different perspective on how to reach that goal. To be clear, the Council proposes to cut commitment appropriations for next year by EUR脗聽1.52脗聽billion. This translates into a EUR脗聽400脗聽million cut for our research programme, Horizon Europe, and a EUR脗聽110脗聽million cut for the Connecting Europe Facility Digital. We also have cuts for the InvestEU Fund, Digital Europe Programme, the Single Market Programme, including SMEs, the Technical Support Instrument as well as for EU4Health.

The Council proposed also a huge cut to Erasmus by EUR脗聽294脗聽million, which is our flagship programme for young people and education. We also see cuts in employment and social innovation, European Solidarity Corps, Creative Europe, Citizens, Equality, Rights and Values and our LIFE environmental programme. Even border protection is affected.

These cuts go against what the Parliament has defended in its guidelines, meaning more investments and financial allocations for the programmes that improve people's lives. We also call upon the Commission to be clear on the negative impact of these measures and to act consistently in enabling us to reach a good outcome during the negotiations.

Together with my colleagues from the Parliament, we prioritise programmes for health, education and youth, support for agriculture and farmers, the need to improve our infrastructure and have a fair, green and digital transition. Defending gender rights, protecting our values and making our fight against corruption more effective. Improving our action at the global level, in particular in our neighbourhood, both east and south: western Balkans, Ukraine and the Republic of Moldova. Protecting our borders and ensuring the functioning of the Schengen Area with Romania and Bulgaria joining the freedom of movement zone by land.脗聽We also call for a better coordination of our social security system to improve intra-European mobility.

Last but not least, we understand the need for solidarity. In the context of the natural disasters that affect our continent, I call together with my colleagues upon the Commission and the Council to allocate additional financial means for all the instruments dealing with such crises, like the EU Civil Protection Mechanism or the EU Solidarity Fund, but also to prioritise investments that enable to reduce the impact of natural disasters. We are confident that the European Union, being based on solidarity, will find the resources for those affected by the floods.

At the end, dear colleagues, I count on your support in establishing a strong budgetary position of the European Parliament. I appreciate the work of our shadows and the good relationship we managed to build.

Dear Minister, I am confident that our negotiations can lead to a good outcome for the people that have so many expectations from us. So let's not let people down.

Dear Commissioner, we do count on your valuable experience in finding the best compromises, hopefully in time, but before the end of the current mandate. We have a lot of work to do together and I'm confident we will find a solution for it.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Niclas Herbst, rapporteur. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Secretary of State, dear Commissioner, dear colleagues, the challenges of the budget have been sufficiently described. You heard about it. Still, I would like to highlight some issues that are important for the European Parliament and for heading seven.

As the rapporteur for the 2023 budget, I have strongly advocated for cybersecurity. And there was a tough discussion then, I remember it very well. It is clear from the requests and of the other institutions, as well as the Council's own budget, that it remains a pressing concern also in 2025. I believe cybersecurity should be addressed across all institutions in a spirit of cooperation, because we all have significant needs across the institutions. So I hope that we will have successful negotiations which will lead to the European Parliament and the other institutions acknowledging the critical importance of providing additional resources to covering cybersecurity needs. I think this is more important than ever, looking at the overall situation.

We must provide our colleagues across institutions with adequate resources, and we consider ourselves as a Parliament to be the advocate of the other institutions as well. This is our part in this procedure. Both Council and Parliament have agreed to give them, sufficient means to protect the interests of Europeans. This is why I have to point out that stable staffing is a very good idea, but has in some institutions reached its limit.

There is an urge to extend for the institutions, especially smaller ones, such as the EDPS. They face severe staff shortages compared to their workload. And of course, if we give them new tasks, we have to also give them the adequate resources.

Also, we cannot allow our colleagues to struggle with security issues, as the EEAS, for example, does while being located in the remote areas of this world. So we must fulfil the security needs to prevent any accidents. Or let me just rather say, incidents. We should not wait for this to happen.

Another critical issue is that the staff situation in Luxembourg must be finally changed. We've been talking a lot about this issue. It has been known for years and extensive conversations among institutions already took place. So Luxembourg institutions must have enough housing allowance to attract qualified staff and work properly for the future of the European Union.

Still, I have to seize the opportunity and stress that we did not take into account the shortcomings of heading seven in the MFF provisions, and we are now having a very difficult situation because of that. Nevertheless, still, I am confident that we budgetary, responsible institutions can work together in the coming months in order to reach a satisfactory result for all institutions and as a result, for the EU citizens. And if we can't save money, at least we can save some time, at least 10 seconds.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Johan Van Overtveldt, voorzitter van de commissie BUDG. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, staatssecretaris, collega芒聙聶s, de rapporteurs zijn al vrij gedetailleerd ingegaan op de verschillende onderdelen van de begroting voor het komende jaar en hebben ook een eerste beoordeling van het standpunt van de Raad gegeven, dus ik ga daar niet verder op in.

Het is nu aan ons om verantwoordelijkheid op te nemen met een verantwoorde begroting die bewijst dat gerichte Europese uitgaven wel degelijk een toegevoegde waarde hebben in functie van onze burgers en hun welvaart. Ik vertel u niets nieuws wanneer ik zeg dat de omgeving waarin we zitten helaas is wat ze is en eerder verder dreigt te verslechteren dan te verbeteren.

We kunnen uiteraard niet voorbijgaan aan het Draghi-verslag, dat een imposante competitiviteitsagenda oplegt en spreekt over investeringsnoden op jaarbasis van 700脗聽tot 800脗聽miljard, en dit voor de verdere uitrol van de reeds overeengekomen EU芒聙聭doelstellingen. Iedereen die de budgettaire toestand van de lidstaten een beetje kent en weet hoe Europese compromissen tot stand komen, weet dat een dergelijke inspanning niet meteen zal kunnen geleverd worden.

Ik daag u daarom allemaal uit om de fixatie op deze cijfers even achterwege te laten en te kijken naar wat we meteen kunnen doen om de kwaliteit van de huidige regelgeving en van de uitgavenstroom te verbeteren. Een van de meest opvallende passages in het Draghi芒聙聭verslag is die waar hij vaststelt dat Europa vier keer zoveel reglementeringen uitvaardigt als de Verenigde Staten. Dat is natuurlijk pure waanzin. Die regeldruk en de daarmee gepaard gaande rapporteringsverplichtingen halen het investeringsklimaat compleet onderuit.

Ik hoop dat we er allemaal van overtuigd raken dat wat wij hier binnen de Europese instellingen 芒聙聰 Commissie, Raad en Parlement 芒聙聰 ook beslissen of doen, het nooit zal lukken om die investeringsstroom tot stand te brengen zolang we er niet in slagen om private investeringen los te weken. We zullen dus onze verantwoordelijkheid moeten nemen op budgettair niveau en de rentabiliteit van private investeringen moeten optrekken. Dat betekent ook een aanzienlijke verantwoordelijkheid voor de individuele lidstaten, want een aantal verantwoordelijkheden zit nog altijd daar. Maar als we het echt menen met ernstige begrotingen en vooruitgang voor onze burgers, dan kunnen we daar niet omheen.

Ik sluit af: wat de Europese Unie op dit moment moet doen, is stabiliteit trachten te garanderen over de beleidsdomeinen heen, gaande van het beschermen van de koopkracht tot het aantrekken en het vasthouden van, zoals reeds gezegd, investeringen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽P脙漏ter Ben脜聭 Banai, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, dear Commissioner, I would like to thank you for this interesting and fruitful exchange. And let me start by answering some critics which were raised by Commissioner Hahn, rapporteur Mr Negrescu, the Chair Mr Van Overtveldt, and it was mentioned by the rapporteur Mr Herbst as well, and that is the cuts of some appropriations.

First of all I would like to emphasise that when we are discussing the EU budget, I think we could not ignore the general economic situation in Europe, which unfortunately shows an almost stagnating European economy.

While on the one hand, today all the Member States and the European institutions as well have to pay much more for debt financing than before Covid or even 2 or 3 years ago. And this is the situation in the case of financing NextGenerationEU as well.

On the other hand, after the temporary relief due to Covid, the renewed economic governance rules have to be applied from now. This means that Member States are now working on serious consolidation of their own national budgets, as the majority of them has bigger debt level than 60脗聽% of GDP, or bigger deficit than 3脗聽% of GDP, or both.

The consequences of these processes that only a very prudent approach could be applied for the EU budget as well. And the Council used this approach when deciding unanimously on its position.

On a positive side. I would like to highlight that the Council has already made some steps towards the European Parliament by increasing several budget lines compared to the Commission's proposal. This the honourable Members of the Parliament generally consider as a priority.

Furthermore, even if the Council applied a prudent approach, I have to stress that the Council's position is still increasing both commitments and payments in the 2025 budget compared to the 2024 level. Again, in these times of budgetary consolidation, they are increasing and not decreasing the overall size of the EU budget for 2025, both as regards commitments and payments.

Today's debate has shown that even though we have different views on specific points, our goal is a common one, to establish a robust EU budget for 2025. I will now await the European Parliament's position on the budget for 2025, with the hope that it will not be too far apart so we can reach a final agreement in November, as mentioned by Mr Hahn.

We hope that we can count on the Commission to act as an honest broker to bridge the gap between our positions and to propose balanced compromise solutions. A smooth and successful negotiation would show citizens that the Union works well, and can provide a budget that meets their needs in these challenging times. Such a budget must be in line with the general economic and budgetary circumstances, and must find the right balance between various political priorities, acknowledging that in budget negotiations, not everything can be a priority. I am confident that together we can provide it in a timely manner.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Die Aussprache ist geschlossen.

脗聽

9. The Hungarian 芒聙聹National Card芒聙聺 scheme and its consequences for Schengen and the area of freedom, security and justice (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Die Pr脙陇sidentin. 芒聙聯 Als n脙陇chster Punkt der Tagesordnung folgt die Aussprache 脙录ber die Erkl脙陇rungen des Rates und der Kommission zum ungarischen System der 芒聙聻Nationalen Karte芒聙聹 und seinen Auswirkungen auf den Schengen-Raum und den Raum der Freiheit, der Sicherheit und des Rechts (2024/2800(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽J脙隆nos B脙鲁ka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, Commissioner, now I stand before this House as a representative of the Council and not as a representative of Hungary. The issue of the Hungarian national card has not figured on the agenda of a Council meeting. The Council has not defined a position on it. I can therefore only express general comments, which are nevertheless relevant to defining the parameters for any debate on this issue.

Like other Member States, Hungary offers several different types of residence permits to third-country nationals. One of these permits in Hungary is entitled 'national card'. As you know, the competence to establish the conditions for admission and legal residence in a Member State is shared between the European Union and its Member States.

However, when granting entry and residence to any third-country nationals under any residence permit category, Member States remain bound by their obligations under EU law, including their duty of loyal cooperation and the need to carry out the necessary checks for entry into the Schengen Area.

Allow me to emphasise that the right of any Member State to issue national residence and work permits that may offer some degree of procedural facilitation or other advantages to certain third-country nationals does not imply, as such, any infringement or circumvention of EU rules. As regards the security of the Schengen Area specifically, all checks under the relevant Schengen instruments will continue to be carried out.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Stella Kyriakides, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, Russia is a threat to the European Union, and that is why today you put this issue on the agenda: Hungary's national card scheme, in particular its extension to Belarusian and Russian nationals.

This scheme was introduced in January, as a type of residence permit first open only to Ukrainians and Serbs. In July, it was extended to citizens of Belarus, Russia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Moldova, Montenegro and North Macedonia. In short, it is now open, on the one hand, to six candidate Member States of the EU, and, on the other hand, to two states hostile to the European Union. That, of course, raises questions, especially because of Russia's invasion of Ukraine, to protect our security, we have been making it harder, not easier, for Russian nationals to enter the European Union.

We suspended visa facilitation with Russia. We issued guidelines to Member States for stricter visa rules and intensive additional scrutiny of Russian nationals at the EU borders. Visas for Russian citizens have dropped by nearly 90脗聽%. We are now currently looking into the implementation by Member States of these guidelines to see if we need further action. We were right to introduce stringent measures, and this is demonstrated by the increasing reports of suspected Russian espionage and sabotage from many Member States.

The exchange this summer of Russian spies and criminals for dissidents and Western journalists illustrates how far Russia is willing to go to infiltrate us. The most striking case: a couple who had been living undercover as sleeper agents. In recent weeks, national intelligence services (Swedish, Norwegian) have warned that the risk of Russian sabotage is on the rise. At a time like this, making it easier for citizens of two hostile states to enter the EU raises serious questions.

That's why Commissioner Johansson asked Hungary for an explanation. On 1脗聽August, she sent her first letter to the Hungarian Minister of the Interior, reminding Hungary of the need for enhanced scrutiny and vigilance regarding Russian nationals and asking a series of questions. We received a reply on 21脗聽August and two key questions remain.

First, why does the Hungarian Government consider it necessary or appropriate to give Russians easy access to the EU in the current political situation? Why can these workers not be found in the EU or through partnership with other cooperative third countries? Second, it appears that the checks on Russian citizens are no different than those for other nationalities, despite the increased risk. It's unclear how the additional security risks posed by Russian citizens are taken into account when assessing their application for the national cards.

Two weeks ago, the Commissioner sent a second letter to the Hungarian authorities to ask precisely this question. Last week, Commissioner Johansson and the Hungarian Minister of the Interior spoke about this issue, and he provided some clarifications. The Commission is now waiting for the written explanations.

Honourable Members, Belarus and Russia threaten the security of the EU: instrumentalisation of migrants, disinformation, infiltration, sabotage and espionage. So I thank you for putting this important topic on the agenda today, and I am very much looking forward to this debate.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomas Tob脙漏, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, Minister, Russia and Belarus pose a serious threat to our European security. They are hostile state actors responsible for countless hybrid attacks, and the only aim is to destabilise and undermine the security of Europe. And of course, we have the awful and horrific war of aggression against Ukraine from Russia.

Against this background, it is deeply concerning and unacceptable that the Hungarian Government has decided to extend the national card scheme to Russian and Belarusian citizens. I must say that it fits very well into the patterns that we have seen from the Hungarian Government. Even the former Trump administration limited their visa-waiver programme due to security reasons against Hungary. That says a lot, I would say.

In the presidency from the Hungarian Government, it is made clear that the objective is to strengthen the security of the European Union and the citizens. And I must say I support that. But that must also lead that you actually act in that way. And I think it is clear for the Hungarian citizens' security, for Europeans' security, that we make sure that we defend our interests, not the interests of Russia.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Catarina Mendes, em nome do Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, Senhor Representante do Conselho, julgo que o momento 脙漏 particularmente dif脙颅cil. E 脙漏 particularmente dif脙颅cil porque ainda vivemos a invas脙拢o do territ脙鲁rio da Ucr脙垄nia por parte da R脙潞ssia.

Por conseguinte, neste quadro, a recente decis脙拢o das autoridades h脙潞ngaras de facilita脙搂脙拢o do acesso da R脙潞ssia e da Bielorr脙潞ssia ao espa脙搂o Schengen, imediatamente ap脙鲁s a visita do primeiro-ministro Viktor Orb脙隆n a Moscovo, 脙漏 perigosa, preocupante e contraria os princ脙颅pios de coopera脙搂脙拢o a que todos os Estados-Membros da Uni脙拢o Europeia est脙拢o obrigados.

H脙隆 mais de dez anos, assistimos, com alguma perplexidade aos atropelos que a Hungria tem vindo a fazer ao Estado de direito e ao respeito pelos direitos humanos. A decis脙拢o de facilita脙搂脙拢o de vistos que 脙漏 apresentada, quando, em paralelo, as autoridades h脙潞ngaras decidiram retirar o direito 脙聽 habita脙搂脙拢o a refugiados ucranianos, n脙拢o deixa de ser extraordin脙隆ria e dif脙颅cil de compreender. 脙聣 verdadeiramente um claro exemplo de profunda falta de humanismo e de solidariedade.

Mas, no que ao 脗芦cart脙拢o nacional脗禄 da Hungria diz respeito, bem como 脙聽 simplifica脙搂脙拢o dos procedimentos para a obten脙搂脙拢o de vistos para 脗芦trabalhadores convidados脗禄 vindos da R脙潞ssia e da Bielorr脙潞ssia, devemos atentar nas palavras da comiss脙隆ria Johansson, mas tamb脙漏m naquilo que foi, agora mesmo, aqui dito: 脗芦A R脙潞ssia 脙漏 uma amea脙搂a 脙聽 seguran脙搂a. Precisamos de mais e n脙拢o de menos vigil脙垄ncia. Permitir que os potenciais espi脙碌es ou sabotadores russos tenham acesso [livre] ao espa脙搂o da Uni脙拢o Europeia compromet[e] a seguran脙搂a de todos n脙鲁s.脗禄

脙聣 disto que estamos a falar e 脙漏 esta a explica脙搂脙拢o que o governo h脙潞ngaro deve dar. O seu 脗芦cart脙拢o nacional脗禄 pode p脙麓r em causa o quadro coletivo vigente desde a invas脙拢o da Ucr脙垄nia, com a suspens脙拢o, no ver脙拢o de 2022, do acordo sobre a facilita脙搂脙拢o da emiss脙拢o de vistos com a R脙潞ssia e a intensifica脙搂脙拢o do controlo de futuros pedidos.

Gostaria, assim, Caros Colegas, Senhora Presidente, Senhora Representante da Comiss脙拢o, de instar o pr脙鲁ximo col脙漏gio de comiss脙隆rios a prosseguir os esfor脙搂os da comiss脙隆ria Johansson, que ainda a semana passada garantiu 芒聙聯 e ainda hoje ouvimos esta garantia 芒聙聯 que a Comiss脙拢o continuar脙隆 a avaliar, de muito perto, o comportamento da Hungria neste 脙垄mbito e a aplicar medidas que garantam a seguran脙搂a da Uni脙拢o Europeia e de todos os seus cidad脙拢os.

N脙拢o 脙漏 f脙隆cil este debate porque n脙拢o devemos tomar um Estado-Membro como um todo. Mas as atitudes e as decis脙碌es de um governo, que podem p脙麓r em causa a seguran脙搂a da Uni脙拢o Europeia, merecem a aten脙搂脙拢o deste plen脙隆rio.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Andr脙隆s L脙隆szl脙鲁, on behalf of the PfE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, there are some people in this House who always point the finger at Hungary. The same applies when it comes to visas issued to Russian citizens. But I'll let the facts speak for themselves.

In 2021, before the war in Ukraine began, EU countries issued 536脗聽241 visas to Russian citizens. In 2022, the year the war began, EU countries issued 687脗聽239 visas to Russian citizens. That's 150脗聽000 more visas into the Schengen zone.

Do you know how many debates the European Parliament held about this? Zero.脗聽In 2023, France issued 99脗聽517 visas to Russian citizens. Do you know how many debates were held in the European Parliament? Zero. In 2023, Spain issued 92脗聽414 visas to Russian citizens. Do you know how many debates there were in the European Parliament? Zero. In 2023, Greece issued 54脗聽289 visas to Russian citizens. Do you know how many debates there were in the European Parliament? Zero.

On 1脗聽January脗聽2023, there were 711脗聽035 Russian citizens legally residing in the European Union. Of those, 259脗聽517 in Germany. Out of those more than 700脗聽000 Russians in the EU, do you know how many reside in Hungary? Anyone? No? Less than 1% 芒聙聯 less than 1%! In the national card scheme in July, Hungary issued a total of five permits to Russian citizens. In August? Five permits. And yet, this is already the second time we're debating this on the European Parliament's agenda 芒聙聯 first in the LIBE Committee, now here in the plenary. These are the facts. I'll let you draw the conclusions.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maciej W脛聟sik, w imieniu grupy ECR. 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo! Nie ulega w脛聟tpliwo脜聸ci, 脜录e nale脜录y z ca脜聜脛聟 moc脛聟 sprzeciwi脛聡 si脛聶 wszelkim u脜聜atwieniom wjazdu obywateli Federacji Rosyjskiej do Unii Europejskiej. Nie popieramy tych rozwi脛聟za脜聞 ani na poziomie kraj脙鲁w cz脜聜onkowskich, ani na poziomie Komisji Europejskiej. Dlatego nie zgadzamy si脛聶 tak脜录e, aby Komisja Europejska wprowadza脜聜a jakiekolwiek u脜聜atwienia dla Rosjan. A tak nale脜录y traktowa脛聡 projektowane przepisy kodeksu wizowego, kt脙鲁re znosz脛聟 wym脙鲁g okazywania ksi脛聟脜录eczek wojskowych dla Rosjan ubiegaj脛聟cych si脛聶 o wiz脛聶 Schengen. Ksi脛聟脜录eczki wojskowe w Rosji to niezwykle istotne dokumenty. Na ich podstawie nasze s脜聜u脜录by mog脛聟 identyfikowa脛聡, czy ubiegaj脛聟cy si脛聶 o wiz脛聶 nie ma w swoim dossier s脜聜u脜录by w armii Putina. Nikt z nas chyba nie 脜录yczy sobie, 脜录eby Unia Europejska go脜聸ci脜聜a morderc脙鲁w z Buczy czy z Irpienia albo weteran脙鲁w grupy Wagnera odpowiedzialnych za mordy w Mali czy w Syrii.

Nie widzicie potwornej sprzeczno脜聸ci w tym, 脜录e Parlament Europejski krytykuje W脛聶gr脙鲁w za polityk脛聶 wizow脛聟, a w tym samym czasie Komisja Europejska przygotowuje u脜聜atwienia wizowe dla Rosjan, w tym tak脜录e morderc脙鲁w ludno脜聸ci cywilnej? Ze zdumieniem przyjmuj脛聶 te rozwi脛聟zania, zar脙鲁wno w脛聶gierskie, jak i Komisji Europejskiej. I apeluj脛聶: wycofajcie si脛聶 z nich. Nie wolno nam traktowa脛聡 Rosjan, Rosji i Putina w pob脜聜a脜录liwy spos脙鲁b. Rosja Putina niestety przy wsparciu wi脛聶kszo脜聸ci spo脜聜ecze脜聞stwa rosyjskiego rozp脛聶ta脜聜a krwaw脛聟 wojn脛聶 z Ukrain脛聟, najwi脛聶ksz脛聟 wojn脛聶 w Europie od 1945 r. Rosja celowo destabilizuje granice zewn脛聶trzne Unii Europejskiej, prowadzi na co dzie脜聞 wojn脛聶 hybrydow脛聟 z Polsk脛聟, Litw脛聟, 脜聛otw脛聟 czy Finlandi脛聟. Wysy脜聜a do Europy dziesi脛聟tki tysi脛聶cy nielegalnych migrant脙鲁w, dokonuje codziennych cyberatak脙鲁w na pa脜聞stwa europejskie, dopuszcza si脛聶 licznych akt脙鲁w sabota脜录u.

Dlatego niezb脛聶dne jest nie 脜聜agodzenie, ale zaostrzenie polityki sankcyjnej Unii Europejskiej wobec Rosji w ka脜录dym wymiarze, tak脜录e wizowym. Rosja Putina musi by脛聡 poddana bezwzgl脛聶dnej izolacji. Mo脜录e w脙鲁wczas Rosjanie zrozumiej脛聟, 脜录e nale脜录y sko脜聞czy脛聡 z Putinem i polityk脛聟 mocarstwow脛聟.

I jeszcze jedna refleksja. Musimy sobie zda脛聡 spraw脛聶, 脜录e na tej sali znajduje si脛聶 wiele os脙鲁b, dzi脛聶ki kt脙鲁rym Putin m脙鲁g脜聜 zaatakowa脛聡 Ukrain脛聶. To ci, kt脙鲁rzy zapewniali Putinowi finansow脛聟 kropl脙鲁wk脛聶. To ci, kt脙鲁rzy pozwolili Putinowi na prowadzenie polityki szanta脜录u energetycznego. To ci, kt脙鲁rzy udawali, 脜录e nie widz脛聟, jak Rosja Putina za pieni脛聟dze z paliw kopalnych buduje swoj脛聟 agresywn脛聟 mocarstwowo脜聸脛聡. To ci, kt脙鲁rzy wbrew interesom ca脜聜ej Europy forsowali budow脛聶 Nord Stream. M脙鲁wi脛聶 to g脜聜脙鲁wnie do pos脜聜脙鲁w z Niemiec i ich przyjaci脙鲁脜聜 politycznych. Posypcie sobie g脜聜owy popio脜聜em. Krew gin脛聟cych i mordowanych w Ukrainie obci脛聟脜录a tak脜录e wasze sumienia.

脗聽脗聽Fabienne Keller, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame la Commissaire, Monsieur le Ministre, le 5脗聽juillet dernier, lorsqu'il s'est rendu 脙聽 Moscou, le Premier ministre Viktor脗聽Orban n'est pas all脙漏 en pr脙漏tendue mission de paix. Il a d脙漏roul脙漏 le tapis rouge 脙聽 Poutine et ouvert grand les portes de l'Union europ脙漏enne 脙聽 un pays ennemi. Cette carte nationale permet 脙聽 un nombre illimit脙漏 de ressortissants russes d'entrer en Hongrie. C'est en totale contradiction, Monsieur le Ministre, avec la politique de l'Union europ脙漏enne depuis le d脙漏but de la guerre d'agression de la Russie en Ukraine.

Depuis脗聽2022, nous avons gel脙漏 la facilitation aux visas russes, nous avons durci les conditions d'octroi de visas aux ressortissants russes et nous avons renforc脙漏 les contr脙麓les aux fronti脙篓res. Ce n'est pas qu'une d脙漏cision solitaire d'un dictateur isol脙漏. C'est un enjeu de s脙漏curit脙漏 int脙漏rieure majeur pour tous les citoyens europ脙漏ens.

Votre coll脙篓gue, Mme脗聽la commissaire Ylva脗聽Johansson, a r脙漏alis脙漏 plusieurs actions depuis l'脙漏t脙漏 pour obtenir des clarifications et des garanties de la part du gouvernement hongrois, sans r脙漏ponse cr脙漏dible 脙聽 ce stade, toutefois. J'appelle donc la Commission et les 脙聣tats membres 脙聽 envisager la suspension temporaire du statut de membre de Schengen de la Hongrie, tel que cela est pr脙漏vu 脙聽 l'article脗聽29 du code Schengen. Agissons avec force contre cette menace 脙聽 notre s脙漏curit脙漏 commune.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tineke Strik, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Council, Commissioner 芒聙聯 maybe for the last time, who knows? 芒聙聯 Orb脙隆n made it very clear where his true alliances lie when he extended Hungary's national card scheme to Russian and Belarusian citizens shortly after his controversial mission to Moscow. Of course, this falls under the national competences of the Member States, and a ban on certain nationalities would be discriminatory and run counter to the Treaties. But in the current geopolitical context, with Orb脙隆n's close relationship to Putin, this decree is far from innocent: it is a provocation and yet another blatant example of Orb脙隆n favouring his ties with Russia over sincere cooperation with the EU and with the Member States. So in any case, at least it's key that the Commission insist on thorough security checks.

But in all fairness, the EU cannot pretend to be surprised by this. For so long, the EU has shown far too little teeth in response to Orb脙隆n's all-out destruction of the rule of law. The Commission has been hesitant in launching legal actions and has prematurely unfrozen funding. The Article脗聽7procedure has withered in the Council, and we have let this crisis fester and fester, weakening the core strength of the EU, namely its common values. To the extent that now, the external security threat is infiltrating our own community.

How much more existential does the threat of Hungary's rule of law crisis need to be before we say 'enough is enough'? I would say enough alarm bells have rung now, and the national card scheme is certainly not the only provocative decree adopted by Hungary. Right after his visit to Kiev, Orb脙隆n decided to revoke the housing rights of Ukrainian refugees from so-called 'non-fighting areas', including families with children. They have been evicted this summer, left out on the street in blatant violation of the Temporary Protection Directive.

And yet the Commission has remained completely silent on this. The EU has solemnly vowed to stand with Ukraine and with its citizens. Where is that solidarity now? What message are we sending to Ukrainian refugees, to the world and also to the other Member States if we let this slide? So I urge the Commission to immediately act against this violation and to uphold the harmonised protection standards. We must treasure and defend unanimous solidarity and support of the EU for Ukrainian refugees. We must put our money where our mouth is.

脗聽脗聽Jonas Sj脙露stedt, f脙露r The Left gruppen. 芒聙聯 Fru talman! 脙聞mnet f脙露r diskussionen 脙陇r det ungerska nationella kortet. Ytterligare en provokation fr脙楼n Viktor Orb脙隆n. Ytterligare ett bevis p脙楼 att Ungern aldrig skulle f脙楼tt bli ordf脙露rande i EU:s ministerr脙楼d.脗聽Ungern f脙露renklar f脙露r inresa och boende f脙露r belarusiska och ryska medborgare i den Europeiska unionen.

Det samtidigt som Rysslands fruktansv脙陇rda och olagliga invasion av Ukraina p脙楼g脙楼r med full kraft. Vi vet, det 脙陇r v脙陇l belagt, att Ryssland bedrivit omfattande spionage i ett flertal EU-l脙陇nder och att det finns konkreta risker f脙露r sabotage. Ryssland underbl脙楼ser destabiliseringskampanjer, politiska konflikter och sprider l脙露gner. Detta har gjort att ett flertal EU-l脙陇nder har valt att utvisa ett stort antal ryska diplomater, p脙楼 goda grunder. Det har inte Ungern gjort. De 脙露ppnar ist脙陇llet bakd脙露rren in i den Europeiska unionen.

F脙露rutom det direkt ol脙陇mpliga i detta hotar det ocks脙楼 den fria r脙露rligheten inom Schengenomr脙楼det. Det h脙陇r leder oundvikligen till en diskussion om att frysa Schengenavtalet f脙露r Ungern. Viktor Orb脙隆n framst脙楼r alltmer som Putins trojanska h脙陇st i den Europeiska unionen. Han f脙露rdr脙露jer st脙露det till Ukraina, forts脙陇tter importera gas som finansierar det ryska anfallskriget. Han bes脙露ker krigsbrottslingen p脙楼 plats i Moskva n脙陇r krigsbrottslingen borde sitta i f脙陇ngelse.

Samtidigt ser vi hur r脙陇ttsstaten har urholkats i Ungern. Man uppfyller inte l脙陇ngre kraven p脙楼 en v脙陇l fungerande demokrati. Fru talman! Det 脙陇r dags att anv脙陇nda de verktyg som finns i artikel sju. Dags att frysa allt ekonomiskt st脙露d till Ungern. Dags att dra in Ungerns r脙露str脙陇tt i ministerr脙楼det och s脙陇tta press p脙楼 landet. Det f脙楼r vara nog med provokationer.

Orb脙隆n har gjort det till en del av sin politiska repertoar och s脙陇ger att ekonomiska migranter hotar s脙陇kerheten. Det 脙陇r oftast varit fel, men nu 脙陇r det r脙陇tt. De h脙陇r ekonomiska migranterna hotar EU:s s脙陇kerhet.

Az uni脙鲁s K脙漏k K脙隆rtya Program sokkal t脙露bb lehet脜聭s脙漏get ad az EU-n k脙颅v脙录lr脜聭l 脙漏rkez脜聭 munkav脙隆llal脙鲁knak, mint a magyar nemzeti k脙隆rtya. Durva ez a kett脜聭s m脙漏rce. Az orosz k脙漏mek besziv脙隆rg脙隆s脙隆ra hivatkozni nevets脙漏ges, hiszen a titkosszolg脙隆latok m脜卤k脙露d脙漏s脙漏nek kutat脙鲁jak脙漏nt biztos脙颅thatom 脙露n脙露ket, hogy az orosz k脙漏mek nem a magyar nemzeti k脙隆rty脙隆val fognak behatolni, ahogy annak idej脙漏n a cambridge-i 脙露t脙露k sem szovjet 脙隆llampolg脙隆rok voltak. Nek脙录nk, magyaroknak lehet probl脙漏m脙隆nk ezzel a szab脙隆lyoz脙隆ssal, van is, de ezt a vit脙隆t majd lefolytatjuk odahaza. Sz脙隆lljanak m脙隆r le r脙鲁lunk, 脙漏s ink脙隆bb fizess脙漏k ki azt a 2 milli脙隆rd eur脙鲁t, amennyibe eddig a magyar 脙漏s az uni脙鲁s hat脙隆r v脙漏delme ker脙录lt, valamint azonnali hat脙隆llyal szabad脙颅ts脙隆k fel a haz脙隆mnak j脙隆r脙鲁 p脙漏nz脙录gyi forr脙隆sokat!

脗聽脗聽Lena D脙录pont (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Madam Commissioner, representatives of the Council, colleagues, I think it's reasonable to set the scene for today's debate. Russia launched a brutal war of aggression against a sovereign country, targeting civilians with bombs, attacking critical infrastructure on a day-to-day basis. Both inside and outside the European Union, we see hybrid warfare being displayed, aggressing our very own citizens and resilience.

In the last month only, we witnessed numerous incidents of sabotage across Europe, followed the Russian shadow fleet on the way around critical maritime infrastructure, and detected highly sophisticated social media campaigns, fuelling populism and radicalisation. Why is it of importance for today's debate?

Because the Hungarian Presidency itself and its programmes have to work on a secure Europe ready to defend itself, which somehow in declaration is a stark contrast to the latest initiative. Because the scheme in question was launched, coincidentally, shortly after the government paid a high-level visit to Russia.

Because helping Russian and Belarusian citizens entering the European Union on the basis of a work visa is not exactly what I would understand as a common understanding of security 芒聙聯 especially not if you somehow don't ask for enhanced security checks reflecting the geopolitical situation, but only for minimalistic checks upon arrival. Or if you argue that you all of a sudden need a large work force, a large number of workers with very specific qualifications, but don't justify a quota or set specific skill requirements to be fulfilled.

So indeed, I would really like not only the Commission to further assess the security risks deriving from that scheme but would also ask the Council to further deploy possibilities to react.

(The speaker agreed to take several blue-card questions)

脗聽脗聽

P脙垄n脛聝 la acest moment v脛聝d c脛聝 dumneavoastr脛聝 confunda脠聸i guvernul cu tot ceea ce 脙庐nseamn脛聝 cet脛聝脠聸enii acestor state. Vreau s脛聝 v脛聝 脙庐ntreb: nu v脛聝 intereseaz脛聝 securitatea grani脠聸elor atunci c脙垄nd ucrainenii, care nu sunt opri脠聸i absolut deloc 脠聶i pot s脛聝 calce, s脛聝 bea la volan - a脠聶a cum se 脙庐nt脙垄mpl脛聝 脙庐n Rom脙垄nia - 脠聶i omoar脛聝 oameni pe str脛聝zi, 脠聶i nu li se controleaz脛聝 niciunul dintre transporturi, asta nu v脛聝 intereseaz脛聝 c脙垄nd e vorba de securitatea Europei ...

脗聽脗聽Andr脙隆s L脙隆szl脙鲁 (PfE), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Colleagues, we haven't heard a single number 芒聙聯 only concerns and accusations. 脗聽脗聽Eero Hein脙陇luoma (S&D). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, Unkarin hallituksen muista EU-maista ja niiden turvallisuudesta selv脙陇sti piittaamaton toiminta on yksi uusi linkki lis脙陇脙陇ntyv脙陇脙陇n huoleen siit脙陇, mit脙陇 meille t脙陇rke脙陇ss脙陇 maassa, Unkarissa, on tapahtumassa. Oikeusvaltiorikkomukset n脙陇ytt脙陇v脙陇t jatkuvan, unionin ulkopoliittisia p脙陇脙陇t脙露ksi脙陇 estet脙陇脙陇n, Ukrainan tukemista vaikeutetaan ja nyt viimeisimp脙陇n脙陇 Ven脙陇j脙陇n ja Valko-Ven脙陇j脙陇n kansalaisille suunnatut nopeat Schengen-viisumit.脗聽T脙陇m脙陇 tapahtuu samalla, kun Ven脙陇j脙陇 jatkaa hy脙露kk脙陇yssotaansa Ukrainassa, kohdistaa unionin j脙陇senmaita kohtaan hybridioperaatioita, kuten olemme my脙露s EU:n it脙陇rajalla n脙陇hneet.

Vapaa liikkuvuus on suuria eurooppalaisen yhteisty脙露n saavutuksia. Se hy脙露dytt脙陇脙陇 p脙陇ivitt脙陇in satojatuhansia, miljoonia kansalaisia ja my脙露s eurooppalaista yritystoimintaa. Mutta vapaa liikkuvuus edellytt脙陇脙陇 my脙露s yhteisi脙陇 s脙陇脙陇nt脙露j脙陇, s脙陇脙陇nt脙露j脙陇, joiden noudattamisesta jokainen j脙陇senmaa vastaa ja joista yhteinen turvallisuutemme syntyy.

Tosiasia on, ettei Unkarin hallitus ole uusia viisumilinjauksia tehdess脙陇脙陇n ottanut huomioon EU:n yhteisi脙陇 turvallisuusintressej脙陇. On murtautumisia t脙陇rkeiden kohteiden l脙陇helle, droonilenn脙陇tyksi脙陇 ja suoranaisia v脙陇kivallantekoja.

Riskit eiv脙陇t siis ole v脙陇hentyneet vaan lis脙陇脙陇ntyneet. Siksi ydinkysymys on, ett脙陇 Unkarin t脙陇ytyy tehd脙陇 turvallisuusarviot my脙露nt脙陇ess脙陇脙陇n viisumeita. Ei kysymys ole lukum脙陇脙陇rist脙陇, vaan siit脙陇, ett脙陇 riskit arvioidaan ja muut j脙陇senmaat voivat luottaa siihen, ett脙陇 Unkarin kautta tulevat kansalaiset ovat tarkistettuja eiv脙陇tk脙陇 muodosta uhkaa muiden EU-maiden kansalaisille.

脗聽脗聽Fabrice Leggeri (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, Madame la Commissaire, le permis de s脙漏jour et de travail mis en place par la Hongrie pour attirer la main-d'脜聯uvre 脙漏trang脙篓re de l'ext脙漏rieur de l'Union europ脙漏enne suscite un d脙漏bat aujourd'hui. Or, ce d脙漏bat montre la duplicit脙漏 de la Commission europ脙漏enne et de certains groupes politiques lorsqu'on parle de l'immigration et de la s脙漏curit脙漏 des fronti脙篓res ext脙漏rieures de l'espace Schengen.

En commission parlementaire脗聽LIBE, il y a quelques jours, la Commission a admis que le syst脙篓me hongrois 脙漏tait techniquement conforme au droit de l'Union europ脙漏enne et dans ce contexte, Mme脗聽la commissaire Ylva脗聽Johansson a r脙漏p脙漏t脙漏 plusieurs fois que la question, pour elle, 脙漏tait celle de la confiance que l'on pouvait accorder ou non aux autorit脙漏s hongroises. Cette question de la confiance est 脙漏minemment politique.

Alors je la retourne 脙聽 la Commission pour savoir si nous pouvons lui faire confiance. Pendant des ann脙漏es, la Commission de Mme脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen et Mme脗聽Johansson nous ont expliqu脙漏 avec la gauche, le centre et leurs amis des ONG, que les migrants qui franchissent ill脙漏galement les fronti脙篓res ext脙漏rieures de l'Union europ脙漏enne ne doivent pas 脙陋tre emp脙陋ch脙漏s de poser le pied sur notre territoire, alors m脙陋me qu'en agissant ainsi, ces migrants violent la loi europ脙漏enne. Le fait d'emp脙陋cher les migrants de violer les fronti脙篓res de l'Union europ脙漏enne constituait, selon Mme脗聽Johansson et ses amis politiques, je cite, des 脗芦push脗聽back脗禄. Des centaines de milliers de migrants sont entr脙漏s chaque ann脙漏e ill脙漏galement en Europe durant les cinq脗聽derni脙篓res ann脙漏es 脙聽 cause de votre id脙漏ologie qui veut nous faire croire, je cite les amis de certaines ONG, que 脗芦les migrants ont un droit inconditionnel 脙聽 poser le pied en Europe脗禄.

Cette id脙漏ologie, votre id脙漏ologie 脙聽 la Commission europ脙漏enne, menace la s脙漏curit脙漏 des Europ脙漏ens car ces franchissements ill脙漏gaux laissent entrer des migrants trop souvent impliqu脙漏s dans des actes criminels et parfois, h脙漏las, impliqu脙漏s dans des actes terroristes contre nos citoyens. Alors je pose la question: qui a pr脙漏f脙漏r脙漏 faire pr脙漏valoir l'int脙漏r脙陋t des clandestins contre l'int脙漏r脙陋t des Europ脙漏ens et contre la s脙漏curit脙漏 nationale de nos 脙聣tats? C'est la Commission de Mme脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen et Mme la commissaire脗聽Johansson. D'ailleurs, elle l'a reconnu le jour de l'adoption du pacte sur l'asile et la migration en d脙漏clarant que c'脙漏tait une victoire pour les migrants. Mais les Europ脙漏ens ont d脙漏savou脙漏 dans les urnes votre politique de soutien aux migrants clandestins.

Alors non, nous ne pouvons pas faire confiance 脙聽 la Commission von脗聽der脗聽Leyen sortante et non, nous ne pouvons pas davantage faire confiance 脙聽 la nouvelle Commission脗聽von der Leyen qui arrive. Les accusations contre le permis de s脙漏jour et de travail hongrois sont un 脙漏cran de fum脙漏e pour faire oublier que le pacte sur l'asile et la migration nuit gravement 脙聽 l'identit脙漏 et 脙聽 la s脙漏curit脙漏 des Fran脙搂ais et des Europ脙漏ens en g脙漏n脙漏ral.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam President, whether acknowledged by them or not, the national card scheme presented by the Hungarian Government poses a severe security threat to Europe.

In late June, the Wall Street Journal reported that the Austrian capital, Vienna, had become a hub for Russian espionage in Europe. Mr脗聽Orb脙隆n should not be so naive to assume that his friendly relations with President Putin will not result in Russia trying to use this visa scheme to make Budapest just another Vienna. Mr脗聽Orb脙隆n's initiative is on track to let the Russians establish another beachhead for its extensive espionage operations right in the middle of Europe.

Good intentions or not, Mr脗聽Orb脙隆n, I think you should strictly reconsider this very dangerous policy. If not, other European countries will unfortunately have to start vetting the people coming from Hungary with extreme prejudice.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Malik Azmani (Renew). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, minister, collega芒聙聶s, Orb脙隆n speelt met de veiligheid van Europa.

Hongarije verleent werkvisa aan Russen en Wit-Russen. Dit geeft hen vrije toegang tot ons grondgebied. Orb脙隆n zet hiermee de deur wagenwijd open voor Russische spionnen. Hij geeft Poetin een oorlogswapen en dat is een grote bedreiging voor onze veiligheid. Orb脙隆n laat hiermee weer zien aan welke kant hij staat in de strijd van Poetin versus het vrije Westen.

De spelregels van Schengen zijn voor iedereen helder. Daarom zijn er nu strengere grenscontroles nodig tussen Hongarije en de rest van Europa. Sterker nog, een lidstaat die onze veiligheid actief ondermijnt, mag niet in het Schengengebied blijven.

Beste commissaris, doe daarom een voorstel aan de Raad tot schorsing van Hongarije uit het Schengengebied. Al doet u dat maar om druk te zetten, want helaas is dat de enige taal die Orb脙隆n verstaat.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Daniel Freund (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Viele von Ihnen haben es vielleicht noch gar nicht mitbekommen, aber Viktor Orb脙隆n hat noch immer nicht bezahlt; letzte Nacht um Mitternacht ist die Zahlungsfrist abgelaufen. Die ungarische Regierung schuldet der Europ脙陇ischen Union 200脗聽Millionen Euro, und jeden Tag wird es jetzt noch 1脗聽Million mehr, weil die ungarische Regierung das Gesetz bricht, weil sie das EU芒聙聭Recht missachtet und das Urteil des EuGH, weil sie denjenigen, die Schutz suchen vor Krieg und Verfolgung, systematisch diesen Schutz verwehrt.

Viktor Orb脙隆n geriert sich ja oft gerne als der starke Mann, der die Grenzen sch脙录tzt, aber es gibt eben zwei Viktor Orb脙隆ns. Es gibt den Grenzen-dicht芒聙聯Stacheldraht芒聙聭Orb脙隆n, und es gibt den Herzlich-willkommen-in-Ungarn芒聙聭Orb脙隆n. Denn bei einer Art von Migration scheint er sehr offen zu sein, also dann, wenn es um Menschen geht, die aus Russland oder Belarus kommen, und mit dieser nationalen Karte 脙露ffnet er T脙录r und Tor f脙录r Spione und Saboteure in die Europ脙陇ische Union. Er untergr脙陇bt die Geschlossenheit der EU gegen脙录ber den Kriegsverbrechern im Kreml, und er gef脙陇hrdet unser aller Sicherheit, weil er gemeinsame Sache macht mit Putin脗聽芒聙聯 hier bei den Visa, aber wir sehen es eben an vielen anderen Stellen auch. Er missbraucht sein Veto f脙录r Putin, und sie klauen zusammen Geld. Was schwei脙聼t schon besser zusammen als gemeinsam Geld zu klauen.

Bei Orb脙隆n geht es eigentlich immer ums Geld, am liebsten nat脙录rlich um unser EU芒聙聭Geld. An seiner Putin芒聙聭Liebe k脙露nnen wir wahrscheinlich nicht viel machen; wir sollten allerdings vorantreiben, ihm sein Veto zu entziehen, mit dem Artikel脗聽7. Beim Geld haben wir noch mehr Spielraum. Da sollte die Kommission endlich in die Wege leiten, nicht nur Teile der EU芒聙聭Gelder einzufrieren, sondern alle EU芒聙聭Gelder, weil alle von Korruption bedroht sind.

Wir sollten nicht l脙陇nger besorgte Briefe schreiben, sondern wirklich agieren, bis der Rechtsstaat wieder funktioniert, bis EU芒聙聭Recht wieder durchgesetzt wird in Ungarn, und bis wir sicher sein k脙露nnen, dass keine russischen Spione oder Saboteure 脙录ber Ungarn in die Europ脙陇ische Union kommen.

(Der Redner ist damit einverstanden, auf mehrere Fragen nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹 zu antworten.)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Milan Mazurek (NI), ot脙隆zka polo脜戮en脙隆 zdvihnut脙颅m modrej karty. 芒聙聯 P脙隆n kolega, vy sa naozaj po脛聧脙潞vate? Tak脜戮e kritizujete Ma脛聫arsko za to, 脜戮e nezaplatilo 200 mili脙鲁nov脙潞 pokutu, ktor脙隆 mu bola udelen脙隆 iba preto, 脜戮e chr脙隆ni svoje hranice pred neleg脙隆lnymi imigrantmi? A z脙隆rove脜聢 d脙隆vate rusk脙陆ch pracovn脙颅kov, ktor脙颅 prich脙隆dzaj脙潞 do Ma脛聫arska na norm脙隆lne pracovn脙漏 povolenia, o ktor脙陆ch v脜隆etko vieme, na t脙潞 ist脙潞 脙潞rove脜聢 ako neleg脙隆lnych imigrantov, ktor脙颅 sa dob脙陆jali cez ma脛聫arsk脙漏 hranice s vyu脜戮it脙颅m n脙隆silia, ktor脙颅 tam prepad脙隆vali 脛戮ud脙颅, strie脛戮ali na hrani脛聧n脙潞 str脙隆脜戮? 脛聦o vlastne od Ma脛聫arska chcete?

Povedzte to 脙潞plne na rovinu. Aby zaplatili pokutu? Aby otvorili hranice dokor脙隆n? Pustili do Eur脙鲁py cel脙潞 Afriku? A n脙隆sledne budete spokojn脙陆? A z脙隆rove脜聢 zak脙隆zali rusk脙陆m pracovn脙颅kom vstup na 脙潞zemie Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nie? 脛聦o je vlastne va脜隆e kone脛聧n脙漏 rie脜隆enie?

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Daniel Freund (Verts/ALE), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Ich schlage vor, dass sich die ungarische Regierung an EU芒聙聭Recht h脙陇lt, an die EU芒聙聭Vertr脙陇ge h脙陇lt, dass sie die Urteile des Europ脙陇ischen Gerichtshofs umsetzt, so wie sie getroffen wurden. Wenn die ungarische Regierung zu einer Strafe verurteilt wird, dann sollte sie diese Strafe zahlen. In der Europ脙陇ischen Union haben wir einen Rechtsstaat. Der funktioniert an den meisten Stellen auch ziemlich gut, mit Ausnahme von Ungarn, wo wir erhebliche Probleme damit haben.

Es ist ja eben angesprochen worden, diese Zahlenfrage. Na ja, es ist eben nicht jeder, der aus Russland kommt, gleich. Bei den L脙陇ndern, die vern脙录nftige Pr脙录fungen machen und zum Beispiel Menschen, die in Russland f脙录r Menschenrechte k脙陇mpfen, ins Land lassen脗聽芒聙聯 das ist in Ordnung. Aber es ist eben nicht in Ordnung, wenn man Leute wie den Tiergartenm脙露rder oder Saboteure und Spione ins Land l脙陇sst. Da habe ich meine Zweifel, ob die ungarische Regierung diese Pr脙录fung ordentlich durchf脙录hrt.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Arno Bausemer (ESN), Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Wir haben gerade den Tiergartenm脙露rder angesprochen: Der ist doch erst von der deutschen Bundesregierung nach Moskau zur脙录ckgeschickt worden!

Sie haben eben gesagt, dass der Rechtsstaat weitestgehend funktioniert. Wir hatten am Montag hier eine Debatte zu den Solingen芒聙聭Morden, die ja Ausdruck daf脙录r waren, dass das Dublin-Abkommen nicht funktioniert. Wie hoch w脙录rden Sie denn den Schaden beziffern, den die deutsche Bundesregierung unter Ihrer gr脙录nen Mithilfe in den letzten Jahren zum Schaden der europ脙陇ischen Bev脙露lkerung angerichtet hat? Beziffern Sie bitte diesen Schaden! Der ist, glaube ich, deutlich h脙露her als 200脗聽Millionen脗聽Euro.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Daniel Freund (Verts/ALE), Antwort auf eine Frage nach dem Verfahren der 芒聙聻blauen Karte芒聙聹. 芒聙聯 Ich glaube nicht, dass es da einen Schaden gibt. Die deutsche Bundesregierung setzt die Urteile des Europ脙陇ischen Gerichtshofs um und ist da auch, vielleicht haben Sie das gesehen, gar nicht zu einer Strafe verurteilt worden. Das hei脙聼t, es gibt da keinen finanziellen Schaden f脙录r das EU芒聙聭Budget. Die ungarische Regierung ist aber verurteilt worden: Da stehen 200脗聽Millionen脗聽Euro auf dem Deckel, und die m脙录ssen jetzt bezahlt werden.

脗聽脗聽Hans Neuhoff (ESN). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Frau Kommissarin, Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Die Neuregelung der Bestimmungen zur ungarischen Nationalkarte wird den nat脙录rlichen und juristischen Personen aus Russland und Belarus k脙录nftig die M脙露glichkeit geben, sich in Ungarn niederzulassen. 脙聹ber 脙陇hnliche Rechte verf脙录gt derselbe Personenkreis bereits in anderen EU-Staaten, z.脗聽B. in Deutschland. Die Sicherheitsbestimmungen der EU werden bei der Erteilung der Einreiseerlaubnis vollst脙陇ndig eingehalten. Die Vorschriften enthalten weder Bestimmungen der Einreise- oder Aufenthaltserleichterung noch umgehen sie Sicherheitsbestimmungen welcher Art auch immer.

Ganz im Gegenteil, sie enthalten sogar mehr Voraussetzungen, als es f脙录r die EU芒聙聭脙录bliche Prozedur notwendig w脙陇re. Insoweit unterlaufen diese Bestimmungen auch nicht die Aussetzung des Visa-Erleichterungsabkommens zwischen EU, Russland und Belarus vom September 2022 und November 2021. Eine Bedrohung f脙录r die Sicherheit der EU oder gar eine Untergrabung der Koh脙陇sion der EU-Au脙聼en-, Sicherheits- und Verteidigungspolitik ist aus diesem Handeln Ungarns nicht zu erkennen.

Ich danke abschlie脙聼end dem Kollegen Leggeri daf脙录r, dass er klargestellt hat, dass die wahre Bedrohung der Sicherheit der EU und ihrer Au脙聼en-, Sicherheits- und Verteidigungspolitik aus dem Handeln der Kommissionen von der Leyen stammt.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Erik Kali脜聢脙隆k (NI). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani predsedaj脙潞ca, ke脛聫 som sa na v脙陆bore sp脙陆tal pani komis脙隆rky Johansson, 脛聧o konkr脙漏tne v nej vyvol脙隆va pocit ohrozenia pri n脙隆rodnej karte Ma脛聫arska, dostal som odpove脛聫, 脜戮e jej hlavn脙陆 probl脙漏m spo脛聧脙颅va v tom, 脜戮e tieto povolenia z脙颅skavaj脙潞 Rusi a Bielorusi, ktor脙颅 dnes pod脛戮a nej bez oh脛戮adu na pr脙颅snos脜楼 kontroly predstavuj脙潞 bezpe脛聧nostn脙漏 riziko pre Schengen.

Samozrejme, zopakova脜楼 tak脙漏 nie脛聧o o neleg脙隆lnych imigrantoch, ktor脙陆ch nekontroluje nikto, by z n脙隆s urobilo extr脙漏mistov a rasistov 脜隆脙颅riacich hate speech. Ak by som aj prijal obavy pani komis脙隆rky, st脙隆le nerozumiem, pre脛聧o dnes rozpr脙隆vame o Ma脛聫arsku a nie o Nemecku, ktor脙漏 minul脙陆 rok udelilo rusk脙陆m ob脛聧anom najviac tak脙陆chto povolen脙颅. Alebo o Po脛戮sku, ktor脙漏 vedie t脙潞to 脜隆tatistiku pri bielorusk脙陆ch ob脛聧anoch.

Mimochodom, Ma脛聫arsko je a脜戮 na 脙麓smom mieste. Pod脛戮a ofici脙隆lnych d脙隆t z Eurostatu sa minul脙陆 rok nach脙隆dzalo na 脙潞zem脙颅 Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nie pribli脜戮ne 720 tis脙颅c Rusov a 85 tis脙颅c Bielorusov. Ale vy ste sa rozhodli, 脜戮e bezpe脛聧nos脜楼 Schengenu ohrozuje pr脙隆ve t脙陆ch ma脛聫arsk脙陆ch desa脜楼 Rusov a 脜隆tyria Bielorusi, ktor脙颅 tento rok z脙颅skali n脙隆rodn脙潞 kartu. Absurdn脙漏!

T脙隆to debata je v skuto脛聧nosti len 脛聫al脜隆脙颅 nechutn脙陆 脙潞tok na suver脙漏nny 脛聧lensk脙陆 脜隆t脙隆t a stelesnenie slovensk脙漏ho pr脙颅slovia: Kto chce psa bi脜楼, palicu si n脙隆jde.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jeroen Lenaers (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, now let's recap. Prime Minister Orb脙隆n visits Putin on 5 July, which was in itself already a blatant violation of the EU's Treaties and our common foreign policy. In more or less the same week, all of a sudden, Orb脙隆n makes it a lot easier for Russian citizens to come to Hungary and, by extension, the EU and our Schengen zone. Less stringent security checks, more favourable conditions. What a coincidence.

And at a time when, as the Commission rightly said, we see more and more reports of Russian sabotage and espionage coming from our Member States when we have a collective effort to reduce the number of Russian visas by 90脗聽%, we have one Member State that opens the back door. What a coincidence. And of course, the Hungarian Government claims that these decisions were made on the basis of labour market and competitiveness considerations, but they fail to give any kind of justification for this. Even in the answers of the Minister they now claim that it's not such an attractive scheme, not many applicants will actually be there. So which one is it?

It is a good attempt to defend the indefensible, because when you look through the Hungarian fog, you can see it very clearly. This is a gift from Orb脙隆n to Putin and a middle finger to the rest of the EU. And it's putting the EU's policy against Russia since the start of Putin's war of aggression at risk, and it is a direct sabotage to all the work we do, while putting the security and the integrity of the whole Schengen area at risk. We cannot accept this. The Commission cannot accept this. We must act and we must act now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Birgit Sippel (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Es geht mal wieder um Orb脙隆n脗聽芒聙聯 heute zur Abwechslung aber nicht, weil wir Ungarns Verst脙露脙聼e gegen die Rechtsstaatlichkeit besprechen, und auch nicht, weil der Europ脙陇ische Gerichtshof Orb脙隆ns Regierung mal wieder wegen ihrer rechtswidrigen Migrationspolitik verurteilt hat. Nein, heute reden wir dar脙录ber, dass Europas selbsterkl脙陇rter Migrationsfeind Nummer eins unbedingt Einwanderung erm脙露glichen und erleichtern m脙露chte. Klingt komisch? Offenbar braucht aber auch Ungarn wie viele andere Mitgliedstaaten Zuwanderung und das sogar in zunehmend gr脙露脙聼erer Zahl, weshalb legale Einwanderungsm脙露glichkeiten f脙录r Menschen aus verschiedenen L脙陇ndern geschaffen wurden. Aber geht es tats脙陇chlich nur darum?

Anscheinend ist es Orb脙隆n ja ein wichtiges Anliegen, legale Einwanderungsm脙露glichkeiten auch f脙录r russische und belarussische Staatsangeh脙露rige zu erleichtern, und 14 solcher Aufenthaltstitel wurden laut ungarischer Regierung seit Juli ausgeh脙陇ndigt. Bemerkenswert ist die zeitliche N脙陇he zu seinem unabgesprochenen Besuch in Moskau direkt am Beginn der ungarischen Ratspr脙陇sidentschaft脗聽芒聙聯 reiner Zufall, sagt sein Europaminister.

Angesichts dieses Besuches, mehr aber noch aufgrund der Haltung Orb脙隆ns zum russischen Angriffskrieg ist die Frage erlaubt, welche Vorteile er sich durch diese Ausweitung der legalen Migrationswege auf russische und belarussische Menschen erhofft. Denn es m脙录ssen schon au脙聼ergew脙露hnliche Umst脙陇nde sein, dass es notwendig sein soll, das Einwanderungsprogramm trotz der geopolitischen Lage und ohne besondere Sicherheits脙录berpr脙录fungen um genau diese beiden Nationen auszuweiten und sie damit genauso zu behandeln wie z.脗聽B. sechs EU-Beitrittskandidaten.

Aber zum Schluss: Bei aller Kritik an Orb脙隆n脗聽芒聙聯 Europa muss pr脙录fen, wie lange wir diesem Treiben noch zusehen wollen und welche weiteren Schritte notwendig sind.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hermann Tertsch (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, Hungr脙颅a preside este semestre la Uni脙鲁n Europea y, sin embargo, no se le ha dado ni una tregua de cortes脙颅a en ese acoso indecente del que es objeto tanto ese pa脙颅s como su Gobierno desde hace cinco a脙卤os. Sin parar, sin parar, acos脙隆ndolo con lo que fuera: todo vale contra Hungr脙颅a, todo, cualquier cosa.

Si saca unos permisos para unas entradas controladas regulares de unos rusos y bielorrusos, es un esc脙隆ndalo monumental, mientras que en Espa脙卤a est脙隆n entrando decenas de miles de personas sin documentaci脙鲁n por todas partes, muchas de ellas causando terror y muerte, y en Alemania y en otros pa脙颅ses est脙隆n causando terror, y eso no parece molestarle a nadie.脗聽Tenemos el colapso de la seguridad en toda Europa, y lo que molesta es que entren unos rusos controlados a trav脙漏s de unos mecanismos de seguridad que tiene Hungr脙颅a y que no afectan para nada a lo dem脙隆s. 脗驴Cu脙隆ntos muertos hemos tenido por los rusos y bielorrusos que han entrado en Hungr脙颅a? 脗驴Y cu脙隆ntos muertos hemos tenido por aquellos que han entrado invitados por la se脙卤ora Merkel y por los Gobiernos alemanes posteriores y han sembrado, como digo, el terror y la muerte?

Creo que es absolutamente escandalosa esta doble vara de medir con respecto a Hungr脙颅a. Hungr脙颅a tiene un Gobierno conservador, y no se le tolera. Tenemos en Espa脙卤a un Gobierno delincuente, un Gobierno que viola la Constituci脙鲁n, un Gobierno que acosa a los jueces, un Gobierno que acaba de sacar unas medidas para estrangular la prensa y toda la libertad de expresi脙鲁n, un Gobierno que maneja los fondos europeos a partir de la Moncloa, con la mujer del presidente reparti脙漏ndolos a amigos a cambio de favores芒聙娄脗聽Eso es Espa脙卤a, y nadie habla de Espa脙卤a ni de las amenazas que supone Espa脙卤a. Y se le da aqu脙颅, ahora mismo, una comisaria con competencias para destruir a脙潞n m脙隆s Europa. Es absolutamente repugnante el acoso que est脙隆 sufriendo Hungr脙颅a.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rihards Kols (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Hungary's decisions to simplify travel for Russian and Belarusian nationals is not just a breach of trust. It is a direct challenge to the security of the European Union. The integrity of the Schengen Area itself may be jeopardised by Orban's unilateral measures at a time when the EU is united in response to Russia's war in Ukraine.

By opening the door to individuals from hostile countries against the EU, Hungary is compromising our collective safety. These relaxed visa rules create dangerous loopholes, allowing unvetted individuals, potential spies, saboteurs and agents of disinformation easy access to our borders.

Let me be clear. This is not about national sovereignty. This is about the security of the EU. If Hungary refuses to align with the common EU approach, I urge its neighbouring countries to reinstate border controls for the time being, prioritising their own national security. Europe's unity must not be sacrificed on the altar of appeasement, especially at a time when the EU is on a high alert for potential acts of sabotage.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jan-Christoph Oetjen (Renew). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, verehrte Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Was da gerade passiert in Ungarn, ist, dass die ungarische Regierung, dass Viktor Orb脙隆n versucht, den Schengen-Raum von innen zu zerst脙露ren脗聽芒聙聯 diese gr脙露脙聼te Errungenschaft, eine der gr脙露脙聼ten Errungenschaften der Europ脙陇ischen Union脗聽芒聙聯, den viele unserer B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rger erleben, nutzen und wertsch脙陇tzen.

Ich glaube, dass mit diesem Alleingang, den die ungarische Regierung dort macht, mit der M脙露glichkeit f脙录r Russen und Belarussen, in den Schengen-Raum einzureisen, Orb脙隆n m脙露glicherweise zum gr脙露脙聼ten Schleuser der Europ脙陇ischen Union wird. Das ist ein inakzeptabler Vorgang, den wir nicht zulassen d脙录rfen.

Wir m脙录ssen klarmachen, dass diese ungarische Regierung diese Regelung dringend zur脙录cknehmen muss. Denn ich m脙露chte nicht aufs Spiel setzen, dass die Ungarn, dieses Volk, das f脙录r die Freiheit vom Sozialismus gek脙陇mpft hat, dass dieses Volk in der Zukunft wieder auf P脙陇sse angewiesen ist, wenn es reisen will in Europa, dass es wieder Grenzkontrollen zu Ungarn gibt脗聽芒聙聯 das alles m脙露chte ich nicht. Aber genau das setzt Orb脙隆n aufs Spiel, und deswegen muss diese Regelung wieder zur脙录ckgenommen werden, damit der Schengen-Raum f脙录r die Zukunft bewahrt wird.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomasz Froelich (ESN). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Die Heuchelei dieses Hohen Hauses ist wirklich beeindruckend. Dort, wo es Terroristen gibt, verschlie脙聼en Sie die Augen; dort, wo es keine Terroristen gibt, wittern Sie pl脙露tzlich ein Sicherheitsrisiko. Nicht in Budapest explodieren die Bomben von Terroristen, nicht in Budapest werden Frauen in Gruppen vergewaltigt, und nicht in Budapest wird das Kalifat ausgerufen, sehr wohl aber in unseren St脙陇dten: in Berlin, in Br脙录ssel, in Paris. Und Sie erdreisten sich ernsthaft, Viktor Orb脙隆n sicherheitspolitische Lektionen zu erteilen? Das ist doch l脙陇cherlich! Die Ungarn wissen ganz genau, wen sie in ihr Land reinlassen脗聽芒聙聯 Sie nicht, das haben die letzten Jahre gezeigt.

Ungarn setzt auf russische und wei脙聼russische Gastarbeiter, um seine Industriepl脙陇ne umzusetzen; das ist Ungarns gutes Recht. In Westeuropa hingegen erleben wir seit Jahren den kompletten Kollaps der inneren Sicherheit, und das liegt nicht an russischen Gastarbeitern, das liegt an illegalen Migranten aus dem afrikanisch-arabischen Raum, die man hier mithilfe von Resettlementprogrammen und Schlepper-NGOs in Scharen in unser Land l脙陇sst.

Lassen Sie die Finger von Ungarn, lassen Sie die Finger von Orb脙隆n! Wer mehr Sicherheit will, muss mehr Orb脙隆n wagen.

脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Dost脙隆l (NI). 芒聙聯 Pan脙颅 p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, v脙隆脜戮en脙颅 kolegov脙漏, zdej脜隆脙颅 v脛聸t脜隆ina kritizuje ma脛聫arsk脙陆 program n脙隆rodn脙颅 karty pro pracovn脙颅ky z osmi zem脙颅, kte脜聶脙颅 proch脙隆z脙颅 p脜聶脙颅snou p脜聶edvstupn脙颅 kontrolou. Kritici p脜聶esto bez dal脜隆脙颅ho p脜聶edpokl脙隆daj脙颅, 脜戮e ti z t脛聸chto pracovn脙颅k脜炉, kte脜聶脙颅 jsou Rusy nebo B脛聸lorusy, p脜聶edstavuj脙颅 bezpe脛聧nostn脙颅 riziko. Toto je uplat脜聢ov脙隆n脙颅 principu kolektivn脙颅 viny. Pro mne vzhledem k historii na脜隆eho regionu zcela nep脜聶ijateln脙漏ho, bohu脜戮el nyn脙颅 mezi tzv. liber脙隆ly op脛聸t b脛聸脜戮n脙漏ho. Tato kritika p脜聶itom p脜聶ich脙隆z脙颅 v 脛聧ase ne脜聶脙颅zen脙漏 a neleg脙隆ln脙颅 migrace, kdy hranice Evropsk脙漏 unie p脜聶ekra脛聧uj脙颅 masy lid脙颅 zcela bez doklad脜炉, zcela bez kontroly. Co脜戮 plat脙颅 i pro ned脙隆vnou vlnu uprchl脙颅k脜炉, kdy se podle zpr脙隆vy 脛聧esk脙漏ho ministerstva vnitra vedle v脙隆le脛聧n脙陆ch b脛聸脜戮enc脜炉 do Evropy dost脙隆vaj脙颅 organizovan脙颅 zlo脛聧inci, tzv. vorov脙漏 v zakon脛聸 neboli mafi脙隆n脜隆t脙颅 bossov脙漏. Takto se mnohem sn脙隆ze ne脜戮 p脜聶es pracovn脙颅 v脙颅za m脜炉脜戮e dostat do Evropsk脙漏 unie jak脙陆koli terorista 脛聧i 脜隆pion. Zanechme proto politicky motivovan脙漏 脜隆ikany Ma脛聫arska a sna脜戮me se rad脛聸ji v z脙隆jmu bezpe脛聧nosti na脜隆ich voli脛聧脜炉, aby ve脜隆ker脙隆 migrace do Evropy byla kontrolov脙隆na alespo脜聢 tak dob脜聶e, jako hodl脙隆 Ma脛聫arsko uplat脜聢ovat sv脜炉j program p脜聶脙颅jemc脜炉 pracovn脙颅ch v脙颅z.

脗聽脗聽Ana Miguel Pedro (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, o sistema do 脗芦cart脙拢o nacional脗禄 h脙潞ngaro representa uma brecha perigosa no escudo que protege a Europa, e as consequ脙陋ncias n脙拢o s脙拢o hipot脙漏ticas.

Quando falamos da Ucr脙垄nia, falamos de um povo que escolheu a democracia, a Europa e o Estado de direito. Quando Viktor Orb脙隆n desvaloriza esta luta, ignora que o ataque 脙聽 Ucr脙垄nia 脙漏, na verdade, um ataque aos valores que cimentam a nossa Uni脙拢o.脗聽Se deixarmos a Ucr脙垄nia cair, enfraqueceremos a nossa pr脙鲁pria democracia.

Enquanto os ucranianos lutam, morrem e resistem 脙聽 agress脙拢o de uma pot脙陋ncia imperialista, a Hungria abre as portas a cidad脙拢os russos e bielorrussos.脗聽Cada soldado ucraniano que se ergue em defesa do seu pa脙颅s 脙漏 um escudo vivo para a Europa. A vit脙鲁ria da R脙潞ssia na Ucr脙垄nia seria a prova definitiva de que as fronteiras na Europa podem ser redesenhadas 脙聽 for脙搂a. E ao mesmo tempo que eles lutam, n脙拢o podemos permitir que se criem brechas para aqueles que querem ver-nos divididos.

O Espa脙搂o Schengen n脙拢o 脙漏 um capricho, 脙漏 um pilar. E o 脗芦cart脙拢o nacional脗禄 h脙潞ngaro transforma Schengen numa fachada vulner脙隆vel, num engano que favorece aqueles que querem explorar as nossas falhas e lacunas.

A ordem internacional est脙隆 em jogo e a Hungria n脙拢o pode estar do lado errado. Neste contexto geopol脙颅tico delicado, este esquema facilita a entrada de cidad脙拢os russos e bielorrussos no espa脙搂o europeu, subvertendo os controlos exigidos pelas normas da Uni脙拢o. 脙聣 uma porta aberta 脙聽 infiltra脙搂脙拢o de espi脙碌es e 脙聽 manipula脙搂脙拢o externa, numa altura em que a R脙潞ssia est脙隆 claramente empenhada em desestabilizar a Europa.

Quando falamos de seguran脙搂a comum, n脙拢o h脙隆 espa脙搂o para concess脙碌es. N脙拢o sejamos ing脙漏nuos. N脙拢o podemos tratar a nossa seguran脙搂a com a leveza de quem acredita que as amea脙搂as desaparecer脙拢o por si mesmas. N脙拢o podemos deixar que o Espa脙搂o Schengen, que simboliza a confian脙搂a entre os Estados-Membros, se transforme num instrumento de manipula脙搂脙拢o pol脙颅tica ou numa fal脙隆cia de seguran脙搂a.

(A oradora aceita responder a uma pergunta 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄)

Should we have a debate about that in this Chamber? So that's a very simple question.

A magyar minisztereln脙露k mindig hat脙隆rv脙漏delemr脜聭l papol, de a val脙鲁s脙隆gban egym脙隆s ut脙隆n gy脙隆rtja a kiskapukat az EU-ba igyekv脜聭knek, val脙鲁di nemzetbiztons脙隆gi 脙隆tvil脙隆g脙颅t脙隆s n脙漏lk脙录l. Itt m脙隆r nem vend脙漏gmunk脙隆r脙鲁l besz脙漏l脙录nk, nem migr脙隆ci脙鲁r脙鲁l besz脙漏l脙录nk, hanem egy s脙潞lyos biztons脙隆gi kock脙隆zatr脙鲁l.

A magyar korm脙隆ny m脙隆sok mellett orosz 脙漏s feh脙漏rorosz k脙漏mek, alv脙鲁 脙录gyn脙露k脙露k 脙漏s h脙隆bor脙潞s b脜卤n脙露s脙露k el脜聭tt nyitotta meg az Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁 kapuit. Azt脙隆n most j脙露n, amir脜聭l besz脙漏l脙录nk 脙漏ppen, a nemzeti k脙隆rtya, amivel szint脙漏n b脙隆rki bel脙漏phet Magyarorsz脙隆g vagy az Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁 ter脙录let脙漏re, csak egy munkaviszonyt kell hozz脙隆 lepap脙颅roznia. Ehhez is csak p脙漏nz kell majd a Magyarorsz脙隆gon vagy az Eur脙鲁pai Uni脙鲁n bel脙录li tart脙鲁zkod脙隆shoz. Csak ez脙潞ttal majd nem k脙露tv脙漏nyt kell v脙隆s脙隆rolni a tart脙鲁zkod脙隆s脙漏rt, hanem ingatlant vagy ingatlanalapot, ak脙隆r a minisztereln脙露k vej脙漏t脜聭l. Vagyis az t脙露rt脙漏nik, ami Magyarorsz脙隆gon szokott lenni: Putyin szolg脙隆lata korrupci脙鲁val is t脙隆rsul.

Az is el脙漏g, hogyha olyan embereknek enged szabad utat az eur脙鲁pai orsz脙隆gokba, akiket 脙聳n脙露k egy脙漏bk脙漏nt saj脙隆t j脙鲁sz脙隆ntukb脙鲁l soha nem engedtek volna be, mert pontosan tudj脙隆k, hogy az orosz vagy a feh脙漏rorosz dikt脙隆tor k脙录ld脙露ttei, j脙鲁l meghat脙隆rozott 脙潞tic脙漏llal 脙漏s feladattal. 脗聽脗聽Csaba Moln脙隆r (S&D), k脙漏kk脙隆rty脙隆s v脙隆lasz. 芒聙聯 Tudja, tisztelt K脙漏pvisel脜聭 脙職r, 脙漏n Magyarorsz脙隆gon 脙漏lek, magyar vagyok, 脙聳nnel ellent脙漏tben, 脙漏s sajnos sok ilyet l脙隆tunk m脙隆r. 脙聳n nem tudja, mert biztos nem hallott r脙鲁la, de 脙漏veken kereszt脙录l magyar p脙漏nzb脜聭l, magyar finansz脙颅roz脙隆ssal 脙漏s magyar diplom脙隆ciai mentess脙漏ggel egy orosz k脙漏mbank m脜卤k脙露d脙露tt Budapesten. 脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Knotek (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, Minister, I would like to thank the Commission, Council and the Hungarian Government, because we have heard that the dialogue is going on, step by step, we believe, to a successful end. And maybe at this stage, this plenary should finish the debate. Because we can ask: 'Tell us in 14 days, in one month what is going on'. But this House requires deeper debate.

Okay, we are in a political House. Let's have a deeper debate. I'm not against it. At the end, we are sharing as 27 sovereign Member States one continent. So if we are with the idea that there is a risk, let's debate the risk and ask how it is addressed. But we shall not apply double standards and one-way democracy and one-way diplomacy.

We have heard so far that these working permits, which are 芒聙聯 in terms of security checks 芒聙聯 on equal footing with other European visas, are now five plus five together. Maybe in years we will be talking about one hundred, a few hundred,脗聽maybe 芒聙聯 and I will just say maybe 芒聙聯 2000. For example, imagine 2000 working permits for Russians and Belarusians. Some of you mentioned, I wrote it down, 'unacceptable and dangerous'. Okay. But where the same people and the same faces have been in 2023, when 220脗聽000 multiple entry visas have been issued to the citizens of the Russian Federation 芒聙聯 100 times more. No one claimed a risk. It's not serious.

Where have you been last week? The Chancellor of Germany announced that he is considering 250脗聽000 working permits for one African country. Maybe 芒聙聯 in your logic 芒聙聯 potential bringers of Sharia law. So where have you been? So please, if you want to have serious debate, please use the data and not double standards.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽
